practicing the principles which he was teaching when he was telling people to

a

speak louder than words I repeat action speaks louder than words I was taking a
Gita class for a family and as part of the discussion
Yukta ahara vihara asya that verse the Bhagavad Gita where Krishna says one
was regulated in his eating sleeping recreation and work and as part of the
discussion I told them that it is very important that we sleep early and wake
up early night time is meant for sleeping and Ayurveda also says that the

good quality rest
early to bed early to rise makes a man healthy wealthy and wise so we were
discussing that and this lady asked me Swami ji we understand it is very

night the kids say abhi toh rati nahi hui hai and they sleep at 1 30 2 o'clock
so then the lady asked me why why is it happening so then I became a little
curious and I asked the husband who's also attending the session I asked them
what time you all sleep so the husband said Prabhu ji hum log kabhi late ho
jata hai baal saade bara ek bach jata hai
so it is very important it is not the words we speak it's not the intent we have children also
they observe rather than the words we speak they observe our actions what we are doing
if we ourselves are sleeping late we cannot expect our children to be
sleeping on time and practice early to bed early to rise

he says this training program at best if we do not

this training is at best an entertainment it's not training it's an entertainment
many times people also go to spiritual discourses they attend the bhagavata

to implement
for example now we have the soulful japa videos on the youtube very nicely done

he stumbles he falls he falls again and again

that's part of the learning process

sushila prabhupada as i mentioned being an acharya he taught by example if he was
teaching about krishna consciousness conviction in krishna being the supreme personality of godhead
bhagavan if he was inspiring people about the importance of krishna consciousness
it was backed with strong conviction in krishna
this was in the year 1972 in los angeles
in iskhan we have the highest body called the gbc governing body commission
so 1972 people the gbc members they were called los angeles and prabhupada was meeting them
And Srila Prabhupada looked at them, some of the GBC members sitting there, right across in that room.
After having talked about Krishna, the importance of Krishna Consciousness, Krishna being the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
the importance of reaching out to people with this message, the message of Bhagavad Gita, message of Krishna Consciousness,
he asked those GBC members, the senior devotees, are you all convinced about the philosophy of Krishna Consciousness?
Are you all convinced? He looked at them and it seems for two minutes, nobody spoke anything. It was pin drop silence.
Again Srila Prabhupada asked, are you all convinced? I want to know.
And then he said, as far as I am concerned, I am fully convinced. Therefore, I am pushing this forward.
For me, it's a fact. Krishna is Supreme Personality of Godhead, it's a fact, it's not a fiction.
Then Prabhupada said, lot of people ask me, Swamiji, do you believe? I do not believe.
Prabhupada says, I do not believe.
It's a fact. There is a difference between belief and fact.
In 1905, man believed that atoms are the smallest particle. But that was a belief.
Later on, it was found that it's not a fact. Man believed that Earth is flat.
But later on, it was not found to be a fact.
Likewise, Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. He is Bhagwan.
Krishna has spoken the words of Bhagavad Gita. It's not a matter of belief. It's not a matter of faith.
It's a fact.
For many people, existence of God, although they may be religious, although they may be pious, although they may be praying to Lord, but it's a matter of belief.
So Srila Prabhupada says, as far as I am concerned, I am fully convinced.
It's a fact. Therefore, I am pushing this movement.
Because of that conviction, just like these days, you know, we have this coronavirus and people talk about COVID appropriate behavior.
One of them being sanitization and then social distancing, maintain a distance, two feet, three feet.
So,
why this distance? Why this COVID appropriate?
Because if you go very close to an infected person, you can also get infected.
So likewise, if a person interacts with a person with strong conviction, strong conviction, that conviction, that faith also is contagious.
It's also infectious.
That's why Srila Prabhupada used to say that we have to be very, very mindful, very, very cautious about whom we associate with.
In fact, he said that one of the most important behavior of a Vaishnava is asatsanga tyaga.
Asatsanga means he should give up wrong association, bad association.
Because association is contagious.
Whatever that faith the person has, whatever his convictions are, unknowingly, unintentionally, it will also influence us.
So, Srila Prabhupada had strong conviction.
When he was talking about Krishna consciousness, it was not, for him it was not, he was not in a mood of accumulating followers.
He was not in a mood of becoming famous.
He was not in terms of, you know, how many followers he got and how many countries he visited.
Countries he traveled and what all he achieved.
He was not concerned about that.
He was convinced that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
The message of Bhagavad Gita is the need of the hour.
And based on that conviction, Srila Prabhupada was speaking, although he was not speaking bombastic words.
Sometimes public speakers, they learn public speaking skills.
The tonality, voice modulation.
Gestures.
So, Prabhupada's words were simple.
But there was transformation in the heart.
Whosoever heard Prabhupada.
And that was because of his purity, because of his conviction.
Srila Prabhupada in 1970, same year, went to Mexico.
And the day, the day one, by that time, you know, as I told you, many people started practicing Krishna consciousness.
And Srila Prabhupada would visit a particular cities.
All the devotees from nearby cities would gather.
Would be eagerly waiting for Prabhupada to arrive.
And when Prabhupada would arrive, there would be kirtans.
And whole day, there will be, you know, discussion.
There will be talks.
There will be lectures.
There will be important appointments.
People would come and meet Srila Prabhupada.
People would at time, Prabhupada would at time go to some universities and address the students.
So, that was it.
So, day one was completely jam-packed.
Day two was for initiations.
So, morning to evening, Prabhupada was speaking to those initiated disciples.
And then the whole ceremony happened.
Lot of devotees had gathered.
And then Prabhupada spoke.
There was a lecture.
And then Prabhupada came back to his room at 9.30 pm.
In the night.
After whole day.
Second day.
Having travelled from the US.
And then the day one, completely jam-packed.
Day two, whole day busy with initiations, meeting devotees, talking to them.
And then at 9.30, when Prabhupada came back to his room,
that was a time when seemingly Srila Prabhupada would be completely exhausted.
Mind you, he was at that time, when he went to America in 1965,
he was 70 years old.
So, 1972 means, add another 7 years, 77 years old.
So, one devotee came and told Srila Prabhupada,
Prabhupada, just now we got an information.
Just now we got a news.
They were trying different, you know, important places where Srila Prabhupada could talk.
Just now we got to know that your interview in a very, very reputed TV channel,
has been fixed.
And Srila Prabhupada, unfortunately, the interview has 1 am, midnight.
I requested them for change of time, but somehow they were saying that this is the only time slot available.
And then Srila Prabhupada asked, what is this TV channel?
So, he says, this is the most prime TV channel and millions of people watch this.
Srila Prabhupada said, let's go.
And it was quite a distance.
So, they drove and then Prabhupada, they reached that studio,
where the interview was supposed to be recorded and then telecasted live.
So, Srila Prabhupada was waiting and then the interview started at 1 am, midnight.
1 am, post midnight.
And then, the interviewer, being a Mexican,
asked many probing questions about why you have come to this country,
what is Krishna Consciousness.
And Srila Prabhupada, you know, with love, with compassion,
with feelings for the suffering humanity, spoke very nicely.
He was quite upfront.
Did not mince words, in the sense, he was not trying to somehow please people,
he was telling the truth.
At times, using words which might even be little painful for a person to hear.
One cannot be happy in this world without being Krishna Conscious.
People in this world are suffering because of want of Krishna Consciousness.
So, he was speaking these words.
So, the interviewer asked lot of questions and then,
about 2 am, the interview got over.
And post the recording, post the interview,
this Mexican, who was apparently a regular Mexican,
must be eating beef and must be a meat eater and non-vegetarian,
a little well-built person,
he came to Srila Prabhupada.
It seems his heart had melted during the interview.
He came to Srila Prabhupada.
He folded his hands.
There were tears in his eyes.
And said,
Swamiji,
please bless me and my family.
Generally, these,
you know, they are quite,
since they interview many people and all,
they are quite hard-hearted.
They don't just, they are not very emotional in that sense.
Because they come across so many people,
so many situations,
so many different kinds of news,
so many upheavals, ups and downs.
So, they are quite strong in that sense.
But it seems this person's heart melted
after hearing the words of Srila Prabhupada.
Folded his hands and said,
Swamiji, please bless me and my family.
So, that was the potency in Srila Prabhupada's words
and that was because
he was practicing what he was preaching.
He was asking people to be Krishna Conscious.
He was Krishna Conscious.
He was Krishna Conscious.
If he was asking people to
believe in the words of Krishna,
he himself
had hundred times much stronger belief
in the words of Krishna.
Otherwise, those words will not have an impact.
In fact, people will like,
people will even clap,
people may even appreciate,
people may even comment saying that
yes, it was so nice,
we enjoyed it so much.
But the transformation of heart may not happen.
Prabhupada used to say,
many times people attend this Bhagavad Saptah
and at times even people cry.
But once they go back home,
the life remains the same.
There is no transformation of heart.
Falena Paricchati,
which means that
you can judge a thing by the result.
In the end of Bhagavad Gita,
towards the end,
in the 18th chapter,
Krishna says,
now I am going to speak to you
my highest instruction.
And in the 18th chapter,
after having spoken these words,
Krishna tells Arjuna,
Man Mana,
think of me.
Bhav Madh Bhakto,
become my devotee.
We were talking about
example is better than precept.
Because of Srila Prabhupada's conviction,
he leading from the front,
he leading by example,
being an Acharya,
teaches by example,
there was transformation in heart of people.
Krishna Bhakti was implanted in the hearts.
And that's how so many people,
millions of them all over the world,
till date,
have become devotees of the Lord
and continuing to become devotees of the Lord
by reading the teachings,
the words,
the lectures of Srila Prabhupada.
So we will come to the third point.
I request all the devotees to help me
with the first two points.
You can jot down in the live chat.
And then we move on to the third point.
Before that,
we will take a small,
we will interrupt,
we will stop for a while.
We will have this nice kirtan.
And while the kirtan is on,
I request devotees not to move on to any other program.
Please participate in the kirtan enthusiastically
so that we can come back charged spiritually,
cover the third point,
and then take up some more questions.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.

So we'll recap the points which we have so far covered.
The first point was example is better than precept.
Where we talked about how to practice what we preach is so very important.
And we covered the example of
Gandhiji where he told that small girl, small boy about not eating sweet after having practiced that himself.
The second thing was action speaks louder than words.
We can't expect a person to follow unless we ourselves practice it.
In fact our words will not have that power, that potency.
We talked about how purity is a force.
And Shri Prabhupada,
because he had that strong conviction,
his words were quite potent and whosoever heard those words there was a transformation in the heart.
The third principle is judged by your behavior.
I repeat, judged by your behavior.
As practitioners of Krishna Consciousness,
it is very very important for us
to be mindful of our behavior.
How we behave,
how we deal with others,
people are watching us.
Shri Prabhupada said,
Your spiritual master will be judged by your behavior.
This is very very important.
This reminds me of a story.
There was a lady who was working in a company
and these were good old days when
there was a telephone operator
and they had a switch board
and the calls would come
and the telephone operator would connect.
So lot of calls on that particular day,
many people were requesting for calls,
short distance and long distance calls
and she was quite busy.
So the main boss of that company
called this lady, the telephone operator and said,
Can you please connect me to this number?
I have something very important to discuss.
So this lady connected the number
but unfortunately,
when she connected it to that boss,
the main person of that company,
he found that it's a wrong number.
So he called back this lady
and said, looks like you've got the number wrong.
Can you please check the number?
And then he spoke the number again.
Please note down this number
and can you kindly connect?
So this lady connected
but again it was a wrong number.
She connected the wrong number.
He in turn again called and said,
Can you please check?
I think by mistake you have got the number wrong.
Can you please try to connect it again?
So she again connected.
Believe it or not,
nine times she connected the number wrongly.
The ninth time,
this person tells this lady,
somehow this call is very important.
Please try your best.
Please connect it again.
I want to speak to this gentleman.
It is very, very important call.
Can you please try again
and be very careful
and try to get this number right?
So this time the lady called,
connected the number
and fortunately it was correct
and he could speak.
Towards the end of the day,
this whole day,
this lady was very, very apologetic,
feeling very bad.
How could I
do it so badly?
Connected the number wrongly
not one time, not two times,
nine times.
So she was very, very
feeling very, very disgusted with herself.
Towards the end of the day,
she requested,
she called that person,
the boss and said,
Can I, sir, come and meet you?
So he says,
Okay, please come.
This lady walks into the office and says,
I am very, very sorry.
The whole day I was very disturbed
because what I have,
every time I connected wrongly,
you were very patient
and you requested me again
and it so happened
that it happened nine times,
not one time, not two times,
nine times.
Never you spoke rudely.
Never you got provoked.
I couldn't see any anger in your voice.
Every time you kept on requesting me,
Can you please try again?
This time be more careful.
How is it that
you never got angry?
How is it that you were so,
you were like a thorough gentleman?
How is it that
I could see this in your behavior?
Why did you not get provoked?
So he said,
It's all the grace of my Guru
who has always taught
that the tongue should never lose its sweetness.
We should never be rude to anyone.
That's what I have learned from my Guru.
Guess what the lady asked next.
I am sure some of you would have guessed it right.
The lady asked that gentleman,
Who is your Guru?
So this person told the name.
And then he also said,
Fortunately, we are quite blessed
that this week he is in town
and he is conducting a big discourse.
He is giving a big discourse.
Would you like to attend?
So this lady was very, very keen to attend.
She actually went, attended that program
and it seems she also became a devotee.
So he said,
Yes, I would like to attend.
So it's very important that
when we try to practice
the principles of Krishna Consciousness,
we wear a Kanti Mada
or we apply a Tilak,
people get to know that
we are practicing principles of Krishna Consciousness,
Bhagavad Gita.
It is very important.
Sometimes we have seen students,
because we talk about higher taste,
we talk about that
how human life is very precious.
Many times people start
not attending to their responsibilities
sincerely.
They lose taste.
For many students,
studying becomes,
it's not important.
Because somehow
we also say that,
Yes, in human form of life,
Krishna Consciousness is very important.
And imagine a student who has
come to Krishna Consciousness,
who attends our sessions,
and then assuming that his
his performance starts dipping.
Obviously people who are watching
would see that
what kind of philosophy is this?
A person goes
and then his performance dips.
Or many times people start getting up in the morning
quite early.
They've heard Brahmamurta time, the importance.
And then in the office,
whole day a person is very groggy,
sleepy,
at times yawning.
It sets a very bad example.
So two things.
One is,
we have to be careful.
Example speaks,
example is better than precept.
Action speaks louder than words.
We may even want to preach.
We may want to share this message with others.
We have benefited.
We may want others also to benefit.
But more than the words,
we speak,
people are watching our actions.
People are watching our behaviour.
Be it in our family,
with our near and dear ones,
they are watching our behaviour.
Yes, we are attending the classes,
but how that is getting translated
into your behaviour.
And at times we can't blame because
initially when a person,
it takes time to get a proper understanding of
the principles of Krishna Consciousness.
At times our behaviour can be very fanatical.
I remember a person started attending our session
and he somehow got inspired,
Brahma Murta time.
And next day,
you know,
he went and kept an alarm.
He got up at 3.30 a.m.
And the whole family was quite disturbed.
But he was insistent,
no, I will get up.
And for him to get up,
living in a small house,
kept an alarm at 3.30,
disturbing everyone.
We have to be mindful.
People will get alarmed
if we are not very careful in our dealings.
How we deal with people,
what words we speak,
how we behave,
how we act,
we respond.
Prabhupada said,
your spiritual master will be judged
by your behaviour.
Just like this,
we talked about this lady.
So it's very important that
we are mindful that
we take guidance,
how to implement.
It's very important we implement what we learn.
Nothing wrong about having decided that
yes, I should get up early in the morning.
Nothing wrong about,
yes, we should practice the principles of Krishna Consciousness.
But how do we translate in day to day life?
It requires some bit of guidance.
For that,
we can speak to the devotees
whom we are in touch with.
We can,
one of the very important source of
practical guidance is
Srila Prabhupada's letters.
There's a Veda base online.
You could,
Prabhupada's conversations.
So many devotees,
the doubts which we might have
is not something new.
Many devotees in the past
who have started practicing Krishna Consciousness
would have gone through those stages.
So they are,
these questions,
these doubts are asked.
And Srila Prabhupada has given wonderful answers.
So we can read those letters
which are available online.
And we can get many important practical insights
how to practice the principles of Krishna Consciousness.
Now,
it is important that
we preach
or we share this message with others.
Bharat,
Bharat bhumile janma hoile yaar.
This is Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's instructions.
One who has taken birth in
this Bharat Varsha,
which is now,
we call it India.
He has two very,
very important duties.
Janma saarthak kari karo pakar.
He should perfect his life
and then,
help others perfect their lives.
Now, does that mean
that unless we practice it cent percent,
backed with conviction,
backed with full knowledge,
backed with realization,
and example is better than precept,
action speaks louder than words,
we should not share this message with others.
We should wait for one day when
we would have got sufficiently realized
when we would have that same conviction
which the Prabhupada had
and then we can preach.
The answer is no.
Even if we are not fully realized,
even if
we don't have full conviction,
even if we are beginners,
still,
we can help others.
The example is
that a person who is in ninth standard
can always teach.
He is not a teacher.
He has not done B.Ed.
He is not a recognized teacher,
but he can always help a child
in the first standard.
And to preach is not very difficult.
More so if we meet a person
who is ready for it.
In fact, I met a person
who said that I speak to my friends
about importance of being a vegetarian
and giving up non-vegetarianism
with non-vegetarian diet.
But somehow this particular person
is not getting convinced.
Can you please share with me some arguments
whereby I am able to convince him?
So then I asked, I inquired
what are his arguments?
What is he saying?
Why he is not getting convinced?
So we should be very,
it is very important for us to know that
we cannot irrespective of our
irrespective of our arguments,
irrespective of our arguments,
we cannot convince everyone.
Not everyone is ready for it.
You can wake up a person,
but you cannot wake up a person
who is pretending to sleep.
You can take a horse to water,
but you cannot force it to drink.
So there are people who are not at a stage
where
they are fortunate enough
to understand Bhagavad Gita.
They are too deeply engrossed in
Tamoguna and Drajoguna,
completely entangled in material life,
lot of material desires.
They may not have
the right frame of mind to hear
and understand the principles of Gita.
We can as a matter of duty do it.
We can even speak to such people,
but we should not waste our time
with someone who is not wanting to listen.
Forget about practicing,
who doesn't want to listen,
who is too deeply,
very materialistic.
In fact, if we are not careful,
the other way,
we can get influenced by their convictions.
As I said, it's contagious.
So we should be protective.
Of our faith.
And try our best,
as a matter of duty,
to speak to others.
It is not that one fine day
when our actions will be perfect.
Actions speak louder than words
and then and then only,
I will try to reach out to more people.
We should try our best to share with others.
The same example,
what I said,
the ninth standard student
can always help a first standard student.
Srila Prabhupada
was once asked,
Srila Prabhupada,
what would make you happy?
What will make you very happy?
So Srila Prabhupada said,
I will be very happy
if people take up Krishna Consciousness.

Or a particular plant.
Or a particular flower to blossom.
So, likewise, this morning time is very, very conducive for us to chant attentively.
The amount of effort which will be required to control our mind, to avoid distractions will be much lesser.
Therefore, it is advised that we chant in the morning hours.
